items.title: Where scaling sits in a feature-engineering pipeline
items.mermaid:
flowchart TD
  raw["raw + cleaned columns"] --> engineer["feature engineering"]
  engineer --> impute["impute missing"]
  impute --> encode["encode categoricals"]
  encode --> scaleBranch{"distance / gradient / penalty model?"}
  scaleBranch -->|"yes"| scaleNum["StandardScaler on numeric branch"]
  scaleBranch -->|"tree only"| skipScale["skip scaler on numerics"]
  scaleNum --> model["fit estimator"]
  skipScale --> model
items.caption: Scaling comes after engineered numerics are imputed and categoricals encoded; include the scale branch only when the target algorithm is sensitive to magnitude.

items.title: ColumnTransformer branches after feature engineering
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  eng["engineered table"] --> ct["ColumnTransformer"]
  ct --> numBranch["numeric branch"]
  ct --> catBranch["categorical branch"]
  numBranch --> numImpute["SimpleImputer"]
  numImpute --> numScale["StandardScaler optional"]
  catBranch --> catImpute["SimpleImputer"]
  catImpute --> ohe["OneHotEncoder"]
  numScale --> concat["concatenate features"]
  ohe --> concat
  concat --> est["estimator"]
items.caption: Only the numeric branch carries the scaler; one-hot columns typically bypass StandardScaler.
